# @peerlytics/sdk

Query ZKP2P P2P protocol data on Base: rates, orderbooks, maker portfolios, and live activity.

## SDK

Package: @peerlytics/sdk (npm)
Install: npm install @peerlytics/sdk

```ts
import { Peerlytics } from "@peerlytics/sdk";
const client = new Peerlytics({ apiKey: "pk_live_..." }); // required (each key includes 1000 free requests/month)
```

## Response shapes (SDK vs raw HTTP)

The SDK targets the Stripe-style v2 wire format (snake_case fields, Unix-seconds timestamps, `{ data, ... }` envelopes) but exposes an idiomatic camelCase TS surface — methods below return the inner payload directly. If you're calling the HTTP API without the SDK, note:

- v1.0 dropped the `success: true` envelope flag — payloads are wrapped in `{ data, ... }` only. (The SDK already unwraps `data` for you.)
- List endpoints return a paginated object inside `data`, not a raw array:
  - `/deposits` → `{ deposits, count, hasMore, limit, offset, filters }`
  - `/intents` → `{ intents, count, hasMore, limit, offset, filters }`
  - `/activity` → `{ events, count, hasMore, limit, offset, filters }` (iterate over `events`, not the top level)
  - `/market/summary` → `{ markets, count, hasMore, ... }`
- Some timestamp fields (`ApiKeyInfo.createdAt`, `lastUsedAt`, `freeCreditsResetAt`) are now Unix seconds (integer) on v2; the TS type is `number | string` because the v1-compat path still emits ISO strings. Convert with `Number(value) * 1000` when you need a JS `Date`.
- `getTimeseries(...)` returns `{ buckets: TimeseriesBucket[] | null, series: TimeseriesSeries[] | null, ... }` — `buckets` is populated when `groupBy` is unset, `series` when it's set. Default with `data.buckets ?? []` before iterating.

## Required filters (throws `ValidationError` client-side)

- `client.getDeposits(filters)` — at least one of `depositor`, `delegate`, `platform`, `currency`
- `client.getIntents(filters)` — at least one of `owner`, `recipient`, `verifier`, `depositId`, `status`

The SDK fails fast before hitting the network; the raw API returns a 400 `missing_filter` with the same guidance.

## Currency hashes vs codes

On-chain, currencies are stored as `bytes32`. Fields to know on deposit responses:

- `DepositMarket.currency` and each `deposit.currencies[].currency` — resolved ISO code (e.g. `"GBP"`)
- `DepositMarket.currencyCode` and each `deposit.currencies[].currencyCode` — raw bytes32 hash (e.g. `0xc4ae21...`)

If you need your own mapping, `client.getCurrencies()` returns every supported currency with its `code`, `label`, `flag`, and all matching hashes.

## Methods

Analytics:
- client.getProtocolSummary() - protocol MTD/QTD/YTD/all-time volume, liquidity, deposits
- client.getLeaderboard({ limit?, offset? }) - top makers/takers by volume, APR, profit
- client.getProtocolOverview(range) - full analytics overview for mtd, 3mtd, ytd, all

Market:
- client.getMarketSummary({ currency?, platform?, includeRates?, limit?, offset? }) - rate stats per pair
- client.getOrderbook({ currency?, platform?, minSize? }) - live orderbook by rate level

Explorer:
- client.getDeposit(id, { limit?, offset? }) - deposit detail with intents
- client.getDeposits({ depositor?, delegate?, platform?, currency?, status?, accepting?, limit?, offset? }) - query deposits — **at least one of depositor/delegate/platform/currency is required**; returns `{ deposits, count, hasMore, ... }`
- client.getIntent(hash) - intent detail
- client.getIntents({ owner?, recipient?, verifier?, depositId?, status?, limit?, offset? }) - query intents — **at least one of owner/recipient/verifier/depositId/status is required**; returns `{ intents, count, hasMore, ... }`
- client.getAddress(address, { limit?, offset? }) - address profile with stats
- client.getMaker(address) - maker portfolio with allocations and profit
- client.getTaker(address) - taker portfolio: fills, tier, lock score, currency/platform mix
- client.getIntegrator(code, { windowDays? }) - ERC-8021 integrator rollup: volume, unique makers, top markets, recent deposits. `windowDays` is currently materialized for 90 only — omit or pass 90 (any other value throws).
- client.getPlatform(platform, { windowDays? }) - platform rollup: currencies, makers, takers, recent intents. Same 90-day materialization rule as `getIntegrator`.
- client.getDelegate(address) - delegate rollup: rate managers, delegated deposits, PnL
- client.getVerifier(address, { limit?, offset? }) - verifier stats and breakdown
- client.getVault(id, { days? }) - vault detail with snapshots
- client.search(query, { type?, role?, limit?, offset? }) - multi-type search

Each explorer entity above also has a canonical page at peerlytics.xyz/explorer/<entity>/<slug> — the SDK method and the page are siblings.

Time-series (Pro, 20 credits/call):
- client.getTimeseries({ entity: "volume" | "deposits" | "intents", granularity?: "hour" | "day", from?, to? }) - bucketed rollups. Hour-granularity is capped at 2000 buckets/call.

Activity:
- client.getActivity({ type?, depositId?, address?, rateManagerId?, since?, limit?, offset? }) - live protocol events — returns `{ events, count, hasMore, ... }`, iterate over `result.events` (not a raw array)
- client.streamActivity({ type?, rateManagerId?, since?, intervalMs?, limit? }, { signal? }) - SSE real-time event stream (returns ReadableStream<LiveEvent>)

History:
- client.getMakerHistory(address) - maker historical stats
- client.getTakerHistory(address) - taker historical stats

Metadata:
- client.getCurrencies() - supported fiat currencies
- client.getPlatforms() - supported payment platforms

Vaults:
- client.getVaultsOverview() - all vaults with AUM, fees, snapshots
- client.getVault(id, { days? }) - vault detail with snapshots

Account & keys (SDK ≥ 1.0 takes the opaque `id` from `listKeys()`, not the raw key):
- client.listKeys() - list API keys; each entry exposes `id` (sha256 of the raw key) for the methods below
- client.createKey(label?) - create key (hits dedicated POST /account/keys)
- client.rotateKey(idOrKey) - rotate key; accepts either the opaque `id` or the raw key
- client.deleteKey(id) - delete key by `id` from listKeys() (raw key is no longer accepted)
- client.getCredits() - credit balance, free-tier window, and purchase history
- client.createCheckout(pkg) - purchase credits (starter/growth/scale)

Webhooks (SDK ≥ 1.0):
- client.listWebhooks() - list registered endpoints
- client.createWebhook({ url, events, label? }) - register endpoint; returns the `secret` once
- client.updateWebhook(id, { status }) - pause/resume an endpoint
- client.deleteWebhook(id) - unregister

Canonical webhook event names (aligned with `LiveEvent.type`):
- deposit.created
- intent.signaled
- intent.fulfilled
- deposit.rate_updated

Legacy aliases (`intent.created`, `intent.filled`, `rate.updated`) are still accepted on register but normalized server-side. Type: `WebhookEventName`.

## Auth

1. API key: set PEERLYTICS_API_KEY env var or pass { apiKey: "pk_live_..." }. Each key includes 1000 free requests/month. Generate via peerlytics.xyz/developers or programmatically with client.createKey().
2. x402: pay per request with USDC on Base, no key needed

## Errors

```ts
import { PeerlyticsError, RateLimitError, NotFoundError, ValidationError } from "@peerlytics/sdk";
```

Types: ActivityStreamFilters, ActivityStreamEvent (for streamActivity), WebhookEventName, WebhookEndpoint (for webhook lifecycle), TimeseriesData, TimeseriesParams, TimeseriesBucket, TimeseriesSeries (for getTimeseries).

## Examples in this repo

- volume-dashboard.ts: protocol stats terminal dashboard
- rate-monitor.ts: poll rates, alert on threshold
- orderbook-snapshot.ts: multi-currency orderbook depth
- maker-report.ts: portfolio report for a maker address
- integrator-report.ts: ERC-8021 integrator stats (deposits, volume, top markets)
- timeseries-chart.ts: hourly/daily rollups in a terminal sparkbar chart
- live-activity.ts: real-time protocol event stream
- webhook-receiver.ts: HMAC-verified HTTPS receiver for outbound webhooks
- x402-agent.ts: x402 pay-per-request flow walkthrough

## Links

- API docs: https://peerlytics.xyz/developers
- SDK: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@peerlytics/sdk
- OpenAPI: https://peerlytics.xyz/api/openapi
- LLM context: https://peerlytics.xyz/llms.txt
- Protocol: https://zkp2p.xyz
